name: HBV_X_gene_integration_hotspot
description: HBV X gene (HBx) and the adjacent DR1/DR2 region account for the vast majority of HBV-host integration breakpoints in hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC). Integrations cluster around the cohesive end region (nt ~1500-1900) and typically truncate HBx near its 3' end while retaining the N-terminal trans-activation domain. Host targets are enriched for TERT promoter, MLL4 (KMT2B), CCNE1, and FN1 — TERT alone accounts for ~30% of HBV-driven HCC integrations. The 3.5 kb pgRNA's terminal redundancy facilitates microhomology-mediated end joining at the integration site.
expected_copy_count: highly variable, 0-100s of integrated copies per HCC tumor genome
